symbols: Virgin spreading her veil/mantle — the protective intercession of Mary over her people
crown on Mary's head — her queenly authority and heavenly status
kneeling figures beneath — the faithful seeking refuge under Mary's protection
ornate floral garment — the beauty and dignity of the Mother of God
carved decorative frame — the liturgical context of the devotional image
description: A folk-art style icon within a carved wooden frame showing a central standing female figure wearing an elaborate crown and a richly decorated floral garment. She spreads a wide red-orange mantle or veil outward with both arms. Below and beside her, smaller figures in various dress kneel in supplication — including figures with beards and crowns suggesting both clergy and rulers. The background is blue and gold. The carved frame features floral vine decoration. The style is distinctly Ukrainian folk icon tradition with bright colors and flattened perspective.
theological_description: The Pokrova (Protection/Intercession of the Theotokos) is one of the most beloved feast days in Eastern Christianity, commemorating a vision in which the Virgin Mary was seen spreading her protective veil (maphorion) over the faithful gathered in prayer. The icon presents Mary as the supreme intercessor — standing between God and humanity, extending her protection over those who call upon her. The figures kneeling beneath her mantle represent all classes of society — rulers, clergy, and common people — united in their need for divine protection. The theology of Marian intercession rests on the conviction that the mother of God has unique access to her Son: 'They have no wine' she said at Cana (John 2:3), and he responded to her intercession. The spreading veil is a visual expression of the prayer 'Under your protection we take refuge, O holy Mother of God' — one of the oldest Marian prayers in the Christian tradition. The royal imagery — crown and ornate garments — reflects the Orthodox understanding of Mary as Basilissa (Queen) of heaven.

scripture_refs.reason: Mary's intercession at Cana — the biblical foundation for belief in her intercessory power

The woman clothed with the sun and crowned — the heavenly queenship reflected in Mary's crown
